sections.heading: Data Type Limitations
sections.body:
By default, MongoDB Search stops replicating changes for indexes larger than 2.1 billion
index objects on a replica set or single shard, where each indexed document
or nested `embeddedDocument` counts as a single object. This means that your
index remains queryable, but you might get stale results.

If you plan to index fields that might exceed 2.1 billion objects,
where an index object is a top-level document or an embedded document,
use the [numPartitions](fts-index-partition) index option to
partition your index (supported only on Search Nodes deployment)
or [Deploy a Sharded Cluster](create-cluster-sharding).

You can't index fields that contain the dollar (`$`) sign at the
start of the field name.

sections.heading: Syntax
Dynamic and Static Mappings
sections.body:
You can configure MongoDB Search with:

- Dynamic mappings to automatically index fields based on a default or
  configured set of types (`typeSet`)

- Static mappings to only index specified fields

You can also use dynamic mappings with [static mappings](fts-static-mappings). Static mappings override the dynamic mappings
configuration.

sections.heading: Dynamic Mappings
sections.body:
MongoDB Search dynamic mappings allow you to configure MongoDB Search to automatically and
recursively index fields in your data. Fields can be indexed based on
the default set of types or by configuring a `typeSet`.

You can enable or configure dynamic mappings:

- At the root `mappings` level to apply to the entire document.

- **\[Recommended]** Within a `document` field type to apply to a
  specified object.

- **\[Recommended]** Within an `embeddedDocuments` field type to apply
  to a specified array of objects that require element-wise query
  comparisons (similar to `$elemMatch`).

> **Note:** 
>
> Best Practices
>
> Dynamic mappings might result in indexing a high number of unique
> fields, which will occupy more disk space and might be less performant.
> Only use dynamic mappings if you need to index fields that change
> regularly or that are unknown. Always use dynamic mappings within a
> document, not at the parent document level.

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [Configure Regex Naming Pattern](fts-regex-naming-pattern-index)

When you use dynamic mappings to index your data:

- MongoDB Search also dynamically indexes all nested fields supported by the
  `typeSet` in a `document` object in your data.

- If a field contains polymorphic data, MongoDB Search automatically indexes the
  field as all of the types supported by the `typeSet` used in the
  index. If a field contains data of a type not supported by the
  `typeSet`, MongoDB Search won't index that data.

sections.heading: Enable Default typeSet
sections.body:
MongoDB Search uses the default `typeSet` when `dynamic` is set to `true`.
In the default `typeSet`, MongoDB Search indexes the BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) types as MongoDB Search
field types. The following table shows the BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) types that MongoDB Search
automatically indexes as MongoDB Search field types when you use the default
`typeSet`. MongoDB Search also automatically indexes the following BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) types
when they are contained within arrays and objects.

| BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) Type | MongoDB Search Field Type |
| --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- |
| Boolean                                       | boolean                   |
| Date                                          | date                      |
| Double, 32-bit Integer, 64-Bit Integer        | number                    |
| ObjectId                                      | objectId                  |
| String                                        | string                    |
| UUID                                          | uuid                      |
| Null                                          |                           |

The following is the syntax for enabling default type set for dynamic mappings:

```json
{
  "mappings": {
    "dynamic": false
  }
}
```

For index examples that demonstrate indexing all fields using the
default `typeSet`, see [Dynamic Mapping Examples](fts-dynamic-mapping-examples).

sections.heading: Configure a typeSet
Static Mappings
MongoDB Search Field Types
sections.body:
MongoDB Search doesn't support the following [BSON data types](manual/reference/bson-types):

- Decimal128

- JavaScript code with scope

- Max key

- Min key

- Regular Expression

- Timestamp

MongoDB Search automatically stores fields of type [string](bson-data-types-string) on `mongot`. You can store fields of all
supported [supported types](bson-data-chart) on MongoDB Search using the
[storedSource](fts-stored-source-definition) option in your index definition. To
learn more about `mongot` and MongoDB Search node architecture, see
[MongoDB Search Deployment Options](fts-deployment-options).

The following table enumerates the supported [BSON data types](manual/reference/bson-types) and the [supported](bson-data-types) that you can use to index the BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) data types.
The table also lists the [MongoDB Search operators](operators-ref)
that you can use to query the field values.

| BSON Type                  | MongoDB Search Field Type                  | Operators and Collectors                              |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| Array                      | booleandatenumberobjectIdstringtokenvector | Operators that support the data type in the array.    |
| Boolean                    | boolean                                    | equalsin                                              |
| Date                       | date                                       | equalsfacetinnearrange                                |
| Date                       | dateFacet                                  | facet                                                 |
| Double                     | number                                     | equalsinnearrange                                     |
| Double                     | numberFacet                                | facet                                                 |
| Double                     | knnVector                                  | knnBeta                                               |
| GeoJSON Object             | geo                                        | geoShapegeoWithinnear                                 |
| 32-bit integer             | number                                     | equalsinfacetnearrange                                |
| 32-bit integer             | numberFacet                                | facet                                                 |
| 64-bit integer             | number                                     | equalsfacetinnearrange                                |
| 64-bit integer             | numberFacet                                | facet                                                 |
| Null                       | N/A                                        | equals                                                |
| Object                     | document                                   | Operators that support the field types in the object. |
| Object                     | embeddedDocument (for array of objects)    | embeddedDocumenthasAncestorhasRoot                    |
| ObjectId                   | objectId                                   | equalsinrange                                         |
| String                     | string                                     | moreLikeThis phrasequeryString regexspantextwildcard  |
| String                     | stringFacet                                | facet                                                 |
| String                     | autocomplete                               | autocomplete                                          |
| String                     | token                                      | equalsfacetinrange                                    |
| UUID/BSON Binary Subtype 4 | uuid                                       | equalsin                                              |
| Vector                     | vector                                     | vectorSearch                                          |

asterisk These operators do not support an array of strings.

cog MongoDB Search doesn't include a field type for indexing null values because MongoDB Search automatically indexes null values for both statically and dynamically indexed fields.

sun Deprecated. To learn more about the deprecated facet field types and their updated counterparts, see [Comparing Field Types for Facet](fts-facet-type-differences).

star Array of `double` or `int` values.

> **Note:** You can store fields of all supported [supported types](bson-data-chart) on MongoDB Search using the `storedSource` [option](index-definition-options).

sections.heading: Examples
sections.body:
The following examples use the `sample_mflix.movies` collection to
demonstrate how to configure the fields to index using dynamic and
static mappings. If you load the sample data, you can create these
indexes on the collection. To learn how to create MongoDB Search indexes, see
[Create a MongoDB Search index](create-fts-index).
